pr.title: Match several ft, pl, and if functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: PR #2578 matched five functions across ftCo guard, plattack, and if_2FC93, and improved fn_802FDA78 using the same file-local struct typing approach used for un_802FE260. The bot report recorded six new matches and several unmatched-item improvements; review evidence is limited to an approval with no inline comments.

key_files.role: Matched ftCo_80091BC4, ftCo_80092158, and ftCo_80093790 with guard-angle cleanup, color-table byte indexing, and a narrow stack-padding tactic.
Matched un_802FE260 and improved fn_802FDA78 by using file-local struct pointer access, signed sentinel typing, const slot data, and explicit color component writes.
Matched plAttack_8003759C by factoring repeated attack-stat clearing into a static inline helper and using full StatsAttack_Count loops.

what_changed.text: Matched ftCo_80091BC4, ftCo_80092158, ftCo_80093790, plAttack_8003759C, and un_802FE260 according to the PR body and bot report.
Improved fn_802FDA78 in if_2FC93 through the same file-local struct typing used for un_802FE260.
Rewrote ftCo_80091BC4 from helper-normalized and deeply nested temporary code into direct stick angle, guard angle, and stick magnitude calculations.
Replaced M2C_FIELD and M2C_BITWISE color construction in ftCo_80092158 with an offset into Fighter_804D650C and explicit color[0..2] byte assembly.
Added a local u8 _[8] = { 0 } stack object in ftCo_80093790 while simplifying the visible body to direct calls and assignments.
Changed un_804A1F10.x31 from unsigned char to signed char so -1 could be used directly as a sentinel instead of 0xFF comparisons.
Changed un_804DDBA8 to static char const and cast its element address to void* when stored in gobj->user_data.
Factored repeated clearing of plAttackStats groups into clearAttackStats and applied it to attacks, hits, and x358_hits.

smart_moves.text: Used a small file-local pointer, struct un_804A1F10_t* p = &un_804A1F10, to make repeated global field accesses shorter and apparently beneficial for both a matched function and a near-match improvement.
Recovered signed sentinel semantics for x31, allowing comparisons and assignments against -1 instead of unsigned 0xFF.
Replaced generated-looking byte-field macros with normal indexed byte access when assembling an RGB integer for efSync_Spawn.
Used a static inline helper to collapse three repeated attack-stat reset blocks into one reusable source form.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For repeated resets of structurally identical subrecords, a small static inline helper can match while producing more maintainable source than copied blocks.
When a file-local global struct is accessed heavily, introducing a local pointer to that struct can be a viable matching shape and can reduce repeated global access noise.
Sentinel fields should use a signed type when the recovered source naturally uses -1; this can simplify comparisons and assignments without relying on unsigned 0xFF spelling.
M2C_FIELD byte extraction can sometimes be replaced by ordinary indexed byte-array access when the table stride and byte roles are evident in the local expression.

naming_conventions.name: clearAttackStats
naming_conventions.rationale: Names the repeated operation of clearing a plAttackStats subrecord.

naming_conventions.name: p
naming_conventions.rationale: Short local alias for the file-local un_804A1F10 struct, used repeatedly in fn_802FDA78 and un_802FE260.

naming_conventions.name: color
naming_conventions.rationale: Local byte pointer replacing temp_r7-style names for Fighter_804D650C color-table access.

naming_conventions.name: stick_rad, stick_deg, deg_delta, guard_deg, smoothed_deg, stick_mag
naming_conventions.rationale: Semantic locals replace lstick/temp-style intermediates in guard angle smoothing logic.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Narrow stack padding was used in ftCo_80093790 as u8 _[8] = { 0 }.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Stack padding is a matching tactic that needs verifier evidence; the PR body provides broad build/report commands and the bot report shows function match, but no inline explanation for why the padding is required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Removed PAD_STACK(24) from fn_802FDA78 while changing access patterns and local variable types.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: This is evidence for reducing an explicit stack tactic, but fn_802FDA78 was not fully matched in the bot report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used explicit component assignments to HSD_MObj material diffuse fields instead of assigning the whole diffuse color struct.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Improves the unmatched item according to bot output, but not a full match for fn_802FDA78.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Changed loop start indices from 2 to 0 in plAttack_8003759C reset loops after factoring repeated resets into clearAttackStats.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Supported by match report for this function; do not generalize to other stats loops without local evidence.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.text: PR was approved with no review comment body in the loaded slice.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report recorded matched code +2420 bytes, six new matches, and improvements including fn_802FDA78 to 99.25%.

follow_up_queries: if_2FC93 fn_802FDA78 file-local struct pointer p un_804A1F10

notes: Strong evidence for changed files, claimed matched functions, diff-level tactics, bot-reported match deltas, and broad verification commands.
No inline review comments were present; reviewer intent beyond approval is not supported.
The PR body lists configure.py --require-protos, ninja build/GALE01/report.json, and ninja, but the intake cannot independently verify command output beyond the loaded bot report.
The u8 _[8] stack object in ftCo_80093790 is visible in the diff and the function is reported matched, but the slice does not explain why that tactic was necessary.
fn_802FDA78 was improved but not fully matched in the bot report, so lessons from that function are weaker than lessons from fully matched functions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use helper extraction for repeated struct resets when it matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2578 matched plAttack_8003759C by extracting repeated clearing of plAttackStats subrecords into static inline clearAttackStats and calling it for attacks, hits, and x358_hits.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Replace M2C byte-field access with indexed table access when local table shape is clear
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2578 matched ftCo_80092158 by replacing M2C_FIELD/M2C_BITWISE use with a shifted table offset and color[0], color[1], color[2] byte assembly for efSync_Spawn.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Signed sentinel fields can clarify recovered source
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2578 changed un_804A1F10.x31 to signed char and used -1 sentinel comparisons and assignments in if_2FC93.c.

source_update_candidates.title: Stack padding needs narrow justification even inside a matched function
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2578 shows a matched ftCo_80093790 retaining a narrow u8 _[8] = { 0 } stack object. Curator may want to cross-reference this with existing matching-tactics guidance as an example where the tactic is visible but should not be generalized without explaining verifier evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already cover PAD_STACK and padding tactics; this PR may provide a concrete curated example, but the slice lacks author explanation for the padding.

source_update_candidates.title: if_2FC93.c uses file-local un_804A1F10_t typing for UI object state
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2578 used struct un_804A1F10_t* p = &un_804A1F10 in fn_802FDA78 and un_802FE260, contributing to a match for un_802FE260 and a 99.25% improvement for fn_802FDA78.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful path-specific fact for future work in this TU, but should be curated as current/path knowledge rather than promoted from intake alone.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the broad idea that all repeated direct global struct accesses should become a local pointer; this PR supports that tactic only for un_804A1F10 in if_2FC93.c.
Do not infer that u8 _[8] stack padding is acceptable general style; the diff shows it and the bot reports a match, but no explicit rationale is present.
Do not treat fn_802FDA78 as matched; the bot report says it improved from 82.48% to 99.25%, not 100.00%.
Do not infer review discussion or requested changes; the loaded review evidence only contains an approval with an empty body.
